final_answer: Simple ways to lower your risk of COVID‑19 are: wear a mask in public places and when sharing a room or vehicle with others [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00016]; keep at least 1 metre (about 3 feet) distance from other people in public [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00016]; avoid crowded, confined, or poorly ventilated indoor spaces [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00016]; wash hands frequently with soap and water or use an alcohol‑based hand rub [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00016]; keep indoor spaces well ventilated by opening windows or using fans [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00016]; get vaccinated against COVID‑19 to reduce infection risk and severity [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00012]; continue physical distancing, mask wearing, and hand hygiene even after vaccination [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00012]; and seek care early and stay home if you develop symptoms to avoid exposing others [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00021].
